explanations.1: SHELL:=bash sets the default shell that Make will use to execute commands in this file.
explanations.3: COLOR_YELLOW:=33 stores the ANSI color code for yellow text, so we can reuse it without typing the number repeatedly.
explanations.8: This line uses awk (a text processing tool) to parse the Makefile itself and extract help text from comments marked with ##, then prints them in a formatted, colored list.
explanations.13: The printf command with escape codes like \e[1;33m applies yellow bold formatting to the text that follows; \e[0m resets formatting back to normal.
explanations.24: The 'deploy: build' syntax means the build target must run successfully before deploy runs—it's a dependency.
